400.description: Bad request - Invalid query parameters or validation error

post.summary: Complete an OAuth signup from a native app
post.description: Native-app variant of POST /v0/auth/complete-signup. Consumes the pending signup token returned by a native provider-token sign-in for a new user, creates the user and OAuth connection, and returns the opaque session token in the body for `Authorization: Bearer` use. The pending token is single-use and short-lived; unlike the browser flow there is no anonymous-cookie binding (the app has no cookie jar), so the token's secrecy is the binding.

200.description: Pending signup completed; session token returned in the body

400.description: Malformed JSON payload, validation error, or another completion attempt is already in progress

401.description: Pending signup token is missing, expired, or invalid

409.description: The handle, email, or OAuth identity is already in use

500.description: Unexpected database, Redis, storage, or OAuth-related error

post.description: Native-app variant of POST /v0/auth/device/verify. Consumes the single-use token emailed after an app login was held for new-device verification, trusts the device, and returns both the session token and a device-recognition token in the response body (app clients have no cookie jar). The app replays the session token as `Authorization: Bearer` and stores the device token to send in the `X-Device-Token` header on future logins, so this device is not challenged again. The emailed token is the proof, so no session is required.

200.description: Device verified; session and device tokens are returned in the body

400.description: Invalid or expired verification token

500.description: Internal Server Error - Database or session store error

post.summary: Authenticate with email and password (native-app client)
post.description: Native-app variant of POST /v0/auth/login. On success the opaque session token is returned in the response body — for replay as `Authorization: Bearer <token>` — instead of an HttpOnly cookie, because app clients have no cookie jar. New-device verification applies exactly as in the browser flow: the app presents its stored device-recognition token in the `X-Device-Token` header (the app-channel equivalent of the browser device cookie); an unrecognized (or absent) device returns 202 and emails a challenge to complete via POST /v0/app/auth/device/verify. The TOTP branch is identical to the browser flow (202 + temporary token for POST /v0/app/auth/totp/verify). `remember_me` is ignored: there is no cookie to persist, and the server's sliding/absolute session lifetime applies regardless.

200.description: Login succeeded; the session token is returned in the body

202.description: TOTP or new-device email verification is required

400.description: Malformed JSON payload or validation error

401.description: Invalid credentials or this account cannot use password login

500.description: Unexpected database or session store error

post.summary: Sign in with GitHub from a native app (provider-token flow)
post.description: Native-app variant of the GitHub OAuth flow (allauth `provider/token` pattern). The app submits the GitHub access token it obtained through its own in-app authorization. Because a bare access token has no verifiable audience, the server validates it against GitHub's token introspection endpoint (`POST /applications/{client_id}/token`, authenticated with our client id + secret), which confirms the token was issued for our OAuth app — a token minted for another app is rejected. The verified primary email is then taken from `/user/emails`. An existing linked account receives the opaque session token in the body for `Authorization: Bearer` use. A new identity receives a pending signup token (bound only by its own secrecy) completed via POST /v0/app/auth/complete-signup.

200.description: Existing account signed in; session token returned in the body. A NEW identity instead returns 200 with OAuthPendingSignupResponse (profile completion required).

400.description: Malformed JSON payload, validation error, an access token not issued for our app, or no verified primary email

409.description: A local account already uses the same email address

500.description: Unexpected database, Redis, or GitHub API error

post.summary: Sign in with Google from a native app (provider-token flow)
post.description: Native-app variant of the Google OAuth flow (allauth `provider/token` pattern). The app obtains a Google ID token via the native Google SDK and submits it here; the server verifies the token directly (JWKS signature, `iss`, `aud` pinned to our client id, `exp`, verified email) with no server-side redirect, state, or browser-cookie binding. An existing linked account receives the opaque session token in the body for `Authorization: Bearer` use. A new identity receives a pending signup token (bound only by its own secrecy) that must be completed via POST /v0/app/auth/complete-signup.

200.description: Existing account signed in; session token returned in the body. A NEW identity instead returns 200 with OAuthPendingSignupResponse (profile completion required).

400.description: Malformed JSON payload, validation error, invalid ID token, or the Google account email is not verified

409.description: A local account already uses the same email address

500.description: Unexpected database, Redis, JWKS, or Google OAuth error

post.description: Native-app variant of POST /v0/auth/totp/verify. Consumes the temporary token returned by POST /v0/app/auth/login, verifies a 6-digit authenticator code or an 8-character backup code, and returns the opaque session token in the response body — for replay as `Authorization: Bearer <token>` — instead of a cookie. Backup codes are single-use and are removed after successful verification.

200.description: TOTP verification succeeded; the session token is returned in the body

400.description: Malformed JSON payload, validation error, invalid or expired temporary token, or invalid verification code

401.description: A backup code was submitted but no backup codes remain for this account

500.description: Unexpected database or session store error

post.summary: Complete an email signup with a verification token (native-app client)
post.description: Native-app variant of POST /v0/auth/verify-email. Consumes the pending email verification token, creates the user account if the email and handle are still available, schedules background indexing, and returns the opaque session token in the response body — for replay as `Authorization: Bearer <token>` — so the app is logged in immediately without a separate login step.

200.description: Verification token accepted, account created; the session token is returned in the body

400.description: Malformed JSON payload, validation error, or invalid verification token

409.description: The email or handle became unavailable before the account was created

500.description: Unexpected database or Redis error

post.summary: Start an email change for the current account
post.description: Re-authenticates the signed-in user with their current password, verifies that the new email is different and unused, stores a one-time email change token, and sends a confirmation email to the new address.

204.description: Email change token was created and a confirmation email was queued

400.description: Malformed JSON payload, validation error, or the new email matches the current email

401.description: Missing session, incorrect current password, or this account does not have a password

409.description: The requested email address is already in use

500.description: Unexpected database or Redis error

502.description: Worker service rejected the email change job or returned an invalid response

503.description: Worker service could not be reached

post.description: Re-authenticates the signed-in user with their current password, stores the new password hash, and invalidates every other active session while keeping the current session alive.

204.description: Password was changed and other sessions were invalidated

400.description: Malformed JSON payload, validation error, or the new password matches the current password

401.description: Missing session, incorrect current password, or this account does not have a password

500.description: Unexpected database or session store error

get.summary: Resolve the session identity for the gateway (forward-auth)
get.description: Internal endpoint called by the API gateway (APISIX) on every request via forward-auth. It validates the session credential against the session store and the database, then always responds 200 with `X-Auth-Status: authenticated|anonymous` plus the user/session/management identifiers on success. The gateway uses these only to bucket rate limits (per-user vs. per-IP); it never blocks here. The backend does not consume these headers for authorization — each route re-validates the session credential itself.

200.description: Identity resolved; see X-Auth-* response headers

post.summary: Complete an OAuth signup after provider sign-in
post.description: Consumes the pending signup token returned by an OAuth login for a new user, verifies that the token belongs to the same anonymous browser context, creates the user and OAuth connection, and signs the user in by setting the session cookie.

204.description: Pending signup was completed and a session cookie was issued

400.description: Malformed JSON payload, validation error, or another completion attempt is already in progress

401.description: Pending signup token is missing, expired, invalid, or bound to a different browser context

409.description: The handle, email, or OAuth identity is already in use

500.description: Unexpected database, Redis, storage, or OAuth-related error

post.description: Consumes the email change token that was sent to the new address and updates the stored email if that address is still available.

204.description: Email address was updated successfully

400.description: Malformed JSON payload, validation error, or invalid email change token

409.description: The new email address is no longer available

500.description: Unexpected database or Redis error

post.description: Completes a login that was held for new-device verification (OWASP ASVS 6.3.5). Consumes the single-use token delivered to the account email, trusts the device (sets the long-lived device cookie), and issues the session cookie. The emailed token is the proof, so no session is required.

204.description: Device verified; session and device cookies were issued

400.description: Invalid or expired verification token

500.description: Internal Server Error - Database or session store error

post.description: If the submitted email belongs to a password-based account, this endpoint issues a one-time reset token and queues a password reset email. It still returns 204 No Content for unknown emails or OAuth-only accounts to avoid account enumeration.

204.description: Password reset email was queued when the account was eligible

400.description: Malformed JSON payload or validation error

500.description: Unexpected database or Redis error

502.description: Worker service rejected the password reset email job or returned an invalid response

503.description: Worker service could not be reached

post.description: Validates the submitted credentials. If the account has TOTP enabled, this endpoint returns 202 Accepted with a temporary token for POST /v0/auth/totp/verify instead of creating a session. Otherwise it creates a session immediately and sets the session cookie.

202.description: TOTP or new-device email verification is required

204.description: Login succeeded and a session cookie was issued

400.description: Malformed JSON payload or validation error

401.description: Invalid credentials or this account cannot use password login

500.description: Unexpected database or session store error

post.description: Deletes the server-side session identified by the current session cookie and returns a clearing cookie so the client removes it from the browser.

204.description: The current session was deleted and the cookie was cleared

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired session cookie

500.description: Unexpected session store error

get.summary: List OAuth providers linked to the current account
get.description: Returns every OAuth provider currently linked to the authenticated user so clients can show connected login methods and account settings.

200.description: Linked OAuth providers were returned successfully

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired session cookie

500.description: Unexpected database error

post.summary: Unlink an OAuth provider from the current account
post.description: Removes one linked OAuth provider from the authenticated account. This endpoint refuses to unlink the last remaining sign-in method when the account has no password set.

204.description: The requested OAuth provider was unlinked

400.description: Malformed JSON payload, validation error, or unlinking this provider would remove the last sign-in method

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired session cookie

404.description: The requested OAuth provider is not linked to this account

500.description: Unexpected database error

get.summary: Create a GitHub OAuth authorization URL
get.description: Generates a GitHub authorization URL with PKCE and stores a single-use state record in Redis. The state is bound to the current anonymous browser context and to the requested flow, which defaults to login.

400.description: Invalid query parameters

500.description: Unexpected Redis or OAuth URL generation error

post.summary: Link a GitHub account to the current user
post.description: Exchanges the GitHub authorization code, validates the single-use state created by the GitHub authorize endpoint, and stores the GitHub identity on the authenticated account. The state is bound to the same anonymous browser context that started the link flow.

204.description: GitHub account was linked to the current user

400.description: Malformed JSON payload, validation error, or invalid or expired state or code

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired session cookie

409.description: The GitHub identity is already linked to this account or another account

500.description: Unexpected database, Redis, or GitHub OAuth error

post.description: Exchanges the GitHub authorization code and validated state for provider identity. Existing linked accounts receive a session immediately. New identities receive a pending signup token that must be completed via POST /v0/auth/complete-signup. If the profile payload does not include an email, the service fetches verified emails from GitHub.

200.description: GitHub identity was accepted but profile completion is still required

204.description: GitHub identity matched an existing account and a session cookie was issued

400.description: Malformed JSON payload, validation error, invalid or expired state or code, or GitHub did not provide a verified primary email

409.description: A local account already uses the same email address

500.description: Unexpected database, Redis, or GitHub OAuth error

get.summary: Create a Google OAuth authorization URL
get.description: Generates a Google authorization URL with PKCE and stores a single-use state record in Redis. The state is bound to the current anonymous browser context and to the requested flow, which defaults to login.

400.description: Invalid query parameters

500.description: Unexpected Redis or OAuth URL generation error

post.summary: Link a Google account to the current user
post.description: Exchanges the Google authorization code, validates the single-use state created by the Google authorize endpoint, and stores the Google identity on the authenticated account. The state is bound to the same anonymous browser context that started the link flow.

204.description: Google account was linked to the current user

400.description: Malformed JSON payload, validation error, invalid or expired state or code, or the Google account email is not verified

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired session cookie

409.description: The Google identity is already linked to this account or another account

500.description: Unexpected database, Redis, or Google OAuth error

post.description: Exchanges the Google authorization code and validated state for provider identity. If the Google account is already linked, this endpoint creates a session immediately. Otherwise it stores pending signup data in Redis and returns a token that must be completed via POST /v0/auth/complete-signup.

200.description: Google identity was accepted but profile completion is still required

204.description: Google identity matched an existing account and a session cookie was issued

400.description: Malformed JSON payload, validation error, invalid or expired state or code, or the Google account email is not verified

409.description: A local account already uses the same email address

500.description: Unexpected database, Redis, or Google OAuth error

post.summary: Sign in with Google One Tap
post.description: Validates the Google ID token on the server. Existing linked accounts receive a session immediately. New identities receive a pending signup token that must be completed via POST /v0/auth/complete-signup.

200.description: Google identity was accepted but profile completion is still required

204.description: Google identity matched an existing account and a session cookie was issued

get.summary: Issue a Google One Tap nonce
get.description: Returns a single-use nonce bound to the caller's anonymous id. Pass it to Google One Tap initialization; it is consumed during POST /v0/auth/oauth/google/one-tap/login to block replay.

500.description: Unexpected Redis error

post.summary: Resend the pending signup verification email
post.description: Looks up an existing email and password signup that is still pending and resends the same verification token with its remaining validity window. Returns 204 No Content even when no pending signup exists to avoid email enumeration.

204.description: Verification email was resent when a pending signup existed

400.description: Malformed JSON payload or validation error

post.description: Consumes a one-time password reset token, stores the new password hash, and invalidates all active sessions for the account.

400.description: Malformed JSON payload, validation error, or invalid reset token

get.summary: List the authenticated user's active sessions
get.description: Returns every active session bound to the current user, marking the session used by this request with is_current=true. Sessions are sorted by created_at descending.

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired session cookie

500.description: Unexpected session store error

delete.summary: Revoke one of the authenticated user's active sessions
delete.description: Deletes the specified session from the server. The user must own the session; revoking another user's session returns 404. If the revoked session is the one used by this request, the response also clears the session cookie.

parameters.name: management_id
parameters.description: Public session management identifier to revoke

204.description: The session was revoked

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired session cookie

404.description: Session does not exist or does not belong to the current user

500.description: Unexpected session store error

post.description: Stores the first password hash for a signed-in OAuth-only account and invalidates every other active session while keeping the current session alive.

204.description: Initial password was set and other sessions were invalidated

400.description: Malformed JSON payload, validation error, or the account already has a password

500.description: Unexpected database or session store error

post.summary: Start an email and password signup
post.description: Validates the requested email, handle, and password, stores a pending signup in Redis, and queues a verification email. The user account is created only after the token is submitted to POST /v0/auth/verify-email.

202.description: Verification email queued and pending signup stored

400.description: Malformed JSON payload or validation error

409.description: The email or handle is already in use or reserved by another pending signup

500.description: Unexpected database or Redis error

502.description: Worker service rejected the verification email job or returned an invalid response

post.description: Requires a current TOTP code, replaces the stored backup code set, and returns the new plaintext codes once.

200.description: Backup codes were replaced and returned in plaintext

400.description: Malformed JSON payload, validation error, TOTP is not enabled, or the supplied TOTP code is invalid

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired session cookie

500.description: Unexpected database error

post.summary: Disable TOTP for the current account
post.description: Requires a current 6-digit TOTP code or an 8-character backup code. On success, clears the stored secret, enabled timestamp, and backup codes.

204.description: TOTP was disabled and all related secrets were cleared

400.description: Malformed JSON payload, validation error, TOTP is not enabled, or the supplied code is invalid

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired session cookie

500.description: Unexpected database error

post.summary: Enable TOTP for the current account
post.description: Verifies the first code from the authenticator app against the secret created by the setup step. On success, marks TOTP as enabled and returns a new set of backup codes.

200.description: TOTP was enabled and backup codes were generated

400.description: Malformed JSON payload, validation error, setup was not started, or the TOTP code is invalid

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired session cookie

409.description: TOTP is already enabled for this account

500.description: Unexpected database error

post.description: Generates a new TOTP secret, stores it as pending on the current user, and returns a QR code plus otpauth URI. TOTP is not enabled until POST /v0/auth/totp/enable verifies the first code.

200.description: Pending TOTP secret and enrollment QR code were created

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired session cookie

409.description: TOTP is already enabled for this account

500.description: Unexpected secret generation, QR generation, or database error

get.description: Returns whether TOTP is enabled for the current account, when it was enabled, and how many backup codes remain.

200.description: TOTP enrollment status was returned successfully

401.description: Missing, invalid, or expired session cookie

500.description: Unexpected database error

post.description: Consumes the temporary token returned by POST /v0/auth/login, verifies either a 6-digit authenticator code or an 8-character backup code, and issues the session cookie. Backup codes are single-use and are removed after successful verification.

204.description: TOTP verification succeeded and a session cookie was issued

400.description: Malformed JSON payload, validation error, invalid or expired temporary token, or invalid verification code

401.description: A backup code was submitted but no backup codes remain for this account

500.description: Unexpected database or session store error

post.summary: Complete an email signup with a verification token
post.description: Consumes the pending email verification token, creates the user account if the email and handle are still available, schedules background indexing, and issues a session cookie so the user is logged in immediately without a separate login step.

400.description: Malformed JSON payload, validation error, or invalid verification token

400.description: Bad request - Invalid JSON or validation error

403.description: Forbidden - Insufficient permissions or post locked

404.description: Post or parent comment not found

500.description: Internal Server Error - Database or transaction error

post.description: Deletes the requested board comment when the caller has permission to remove it.

500.description: Internal Server Error - Database or transaction error

get.description: Returns a post's top-level comments, or the replies under a comment when parent_comment_id is set, with pagination.

parameters.name: parent_comment_id
parameters.description: `None` lists top-level comments; `Some(id)` lists the replies under that comment.

parameters.name: cursor_id
parameters.description:
Cursor keyed by a comment id (UUIDv7, time-ordered). Omit to fetch the
first (oldest) page from the start of the thread.

parameters.name: focus_comment_id
parameters.description:
Deep-link anchor: return the page window *containing* this comment
instead of paging from a cursor. The comment must belong to the listing
scope (`post_id` + `parent_comment_id`). Mutually exclusive with
`cursor_id`.

parameters.name: cursor_direction
parameters.description:
Pagination direction relative to the cursor. This list reads **oldest-first**,
and every page is returned in that order regardless of direction —
`Older`/`Newer` only choose which side of the cursor to read. Defaults to
`Newer` (advance toward newer comments) when a cursor is set; ignored otherwise.

400.description: Bad request - Invalid query parameters or validation error

500.description: Internal Server Error - Database error

post.description: Rewrites the display order of a board's pinned posts. `post_ids` is the full pin list, top first; positions are derived from the list index. The list must name exactly the board's current pin set — a stale one is rejected (409) rather than partially applied. Reordering neither pins nor unpins. Requires board moderation permission.

get.description: Returns per action notification preference overrides for the current authenticated user.

500.description: Internal Server Error - Database error

post.description: Replaces per action notification preference overrides for the current authenticated user.

400.description: Bad request - Invalid JSON or validation error

500.description: Internal Server Error - Database error

post.description: Updates notification delivery preferences for the current authenticated user.

400.description: Bad request - Invalid JSON or validation error

500.description: Internal Server Error - Database or transaction error

get.description: Returns the number of unread notifications for the current authenticated user.

500.description: Internal Server Error - Database error

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Search query for handle, display_name, or bio. Empty or omitted returns all users.

400.description: Bad request - Invalid query parameters or validation error

500.description: Internal Server Error

get.description: Returns the profile for the current authenticated user.

500.description: Internal Server Error - Database error

delete.description: Deletes the current account after re-authentication (OWASP ASVS 7.5.1). Password accounts must supply `password`; OAuth-only accounts with TOTP must supply `totp_code`. OAuth-only accounts with no inline factor receive a confirmation email instead and the response is 202 (deletion completes at the confirm endpoint).

202.description: Confirmation email sent; deletion pending confirmation

204.description: Account deleted successfully

401.description: Unauthorized - Invalid session or missing/failed re-authentication

500.description: Internal Server Error - Database or session error

patch.description: Updates profile fields for the current authenticated user and returns the updated profile.

400.description: Bad Request - Validation error

500.description: Internal Server Error - Database or storage error

post.description: Uploads or replaces the banner image for the current authenticated user profile. The uploaded file must be JPEG, PNG, GIF, or WebP and is sanitized into WebP before storage.

400.description: Bad Request - Invalid image or validation error

413.description: Payload Too Large - Image exceeds size limit

500.description: Internal Server Error - Database or storage error

delete.description: Deletes the current authenticated user banner image.

204.description: Banner image deleted successfully

404.description: Not Found - No banner image to delete

500.description: Internal Server Error - Database or storage error

post.description: Completes a deferred account deletion for OAuth-only accounts using the single-use token delivered to the account's email address. The emailed token is the re-authentication proof, so no session is required.

204.description: Account deleted successfully

400.description: Bad Request - Invalid or expired token

500.description: Internal Server Error - Database or session error

post.description: Uploads or replaces the profile image for the current authenticated user profile. The uploaded file must be JPEG, PNG, GIF, or WebP and is sanitized into WebP before storage.

400.description: Bad Request - Invalid image or validation error

413.description: Payload Too Large - Image exceeds size limit

500.description: Internal Server Error - Database or storage error

delete.description: Deletes the current authenticated user profile image.

204.description: Profile image deleted successfully

404.description: Not Found - No profile image to delete

500.description: Internal Server Error - Database or storage error

post.description: Bans the requested user account and returns the resulting moderation record.

400.description: Bad request - Invalid JSON or validation error

409.description: Conflict - User already banned

500.description: Internal Server Error - Database or transaction error

get.description: Checks whether the requested user handle is currently available for use.

400.description: Bad request - Invalid handle format

500.description: Internal Server Error - Database error

get.description: Returns the public profile for the requested user handle.

400.description: Bad request - Invalid query parameters

500.description: Internal Server Error - Database error

get.summary: Get a user profile by ID
get.description: Returns the public profile for the requested user ID.

400.description: Bad request - Invalid query parameters

500.description: Internal Server Error - Database error

post.description: Grants the requested role to the target user account.

400.description: Bad request - Invalid JSON or validation error

409.description: Conflict - User already has this role

500.description: Internal Server Error - Database or transaction error

post.description: Removes the requested role from the target user account.

400.description: Bad request - User does not have this role

500.description: Internal Server Error - Database or transaction error

post.description: Removes the active ban from the requested user account.

400.description: Bad request - User is not banned

500.description: Internal Server Error - Database or transaction error

AclGroupListResponse.description: Response payload for listing ACL groups.

AclGroupMemberListResponse.description: Response payload for listing an ACL group's active members.

has_more.description: Whether older entries exist beyond this page.

AclGroupMemberResponse.description: Response payload for one ACL group membership.

AclGroupResponse.description: Response payload for one ACL group.

is_system.description: System groups are known to the code and cannot be deleted.

AclPermissionListResponse.description:
Every permission codename the application defines — what an admin UI can
offer as checkboxes (Django's permission list).

ActionLogAction.description:
Action Log Action enum (stored in action_logs.action field)
Format: "{resource}:{operation}"

has_newer.description: Whether there are newer (more recent) action logs

has_older.description: Whether there are older action logs

ActionLogResponse.description: Response payload for action log response.

deactivated.description: True if the user is deactivated (soft-deleted); handle/display_name stay exposed while the profile is masked.

AddAclGroupMemberRequest.description: Request payload for adding a member (a user XOR an IP/CIDR) to an ACL group.

ip_address.description: IP address or CIDR range member (mutually exclusive with `user_id`)

AppDeviceVerifyResponse.description: Session and device tokens returned to a native-app client after new-device verification.

device_token.description:
Opaque device-recognition token. Store it and send it in the `X-Device-Token` header on
future logins to skip the new-device email challenge on this device.

token.description: Opaque session token. Send it back verbatim in the `Authorization: Bearer` header.

BanUserRequest.description: Request payload for ban user request.

BanUserResponse.description: Response payload for ban user response.

can_delete.description: Whether the caller can delete this comment (author or moderator).

can_edit.description: Whether the caller can edit this comment (author or moderator).

content.description: Raw sevenmark markup (used to populate the edit form).

parent_comment_id.description: `None` for a top-level comment; the thread root's id for a reply.

BoardPermissionsResponse.description: Board-level capability flags for the caller, mirroring the `BoardPermission` rules.

has_more.description:
Whether another page of `posts` exists. Pins are not paged and never
affect this.

pinned.description:
The board's pinned posts in display order, top first. Pins sit outside
pagination: this is the complete set on every page, and none of them
appear in `posts`.

posts.description: One page of the board's unpinned posts, newest first.

BoardPostModerationRequest.description:
Shared request body for board post moderation actions (pin/unpin/lock/unlock).
Each action records a moderation-log entry, so a reason is required.

BoardPostModerationResponse.description: Result of a board post moderation action, echoing the post's resulting state.

BoardPostReorderPinsRequest.description:
Rewrites a board's pin display order.

`post_ids` is the board's whole pin list in the order it should render, top
first; the server derives each position from the list index, so no numbers
are sent. It must name exactly the board's current pin set — anything else is
a stale list and is rejected whole (409) rather than partially applied.
Reordering neither pins nor unpins.

Like every board moderation action, this is recorded in the moderation log
and so requires a reason.

BoardPostReorderPinsResponse.description: Result of a pin reorder, echoing the board's pin list in its new order.

can_delete.description: Whether the caller can delete this post (author or moderator).

can_edit.description: Whether the caller can edit this post (author or moderator).

content.description: Raw sevenmark markup (used to populate the edit form).

can_moderate.description: Whether the caller can moderate this board (pin/lock/remove others' content).

can_write.description: Whether the caller can create a post in this board.

ChangeEmailRequest.description: Request body for starting an email change.

password.description:
Current password (for identity verification). No length policy is applied since it is
only checked against the hash (only the hashing util's byte cap applies). An empty
value simply fails as a hash mismatch.

ChangePasswordRequest.description: Request body for changing the current password.

current_password.description:
Current password. No length policy is applied since it is only checked against the
hash (only the hashing util's byte cap applies). An empty value simply fails as a
hash mismatch.

CheckHandleAvailablePath.description: Request payload for check handle available path.

CheckHandleAvailableResponse.description: Response payload for check handle available response.

CompleteSignupRequest.description: Request body for completing an OAuth signup after a provider login for a new user.

display_name.description:
Display name shown in the UI.

- Length: 1-50 characters
- Unicode letters, spaces, and punctuation are permitted
- Emoji, control characters, and invisible Unicode are not allowed

handle.description:
Unique user handle.

- Length: 4–15 characters
- Allowed characters: `a-z`, `A-Z`, `0-9`, `_`
- Cannot start or end with `_`
- No consecutive underscores (`__`)
- Reserved words are not allowed (e.g. `admin`, `support`, `help`, `system`)

pending_token.description: Pending signup token (returned on OAuth login)

ConfirmAccountDeletionRequest.description: Request body for confirming account deletion via email token.

token.description: The single-use token delivered to the account's email address.

ConfirmEmailChangeRequest.description: Request body for confirming an email change.

token.description: Email change token (the ?token= value from the email link)

CreateAclGroupRequest.description: Request payload for creating an ACL group.

parent_comment_id.description:
When set, this comment is a reply. Depth is capped at 2: a reply to a reply
is attached to the same thread root.

CreateUserRequest.description: Request body for starting an email and password signup.

display_name.description:
Display name shown in the UI.

- Length: 1–50 characters
- Unicode letters, spaces, and punctuation are permitted
- Emoji, control characters, and invisible Unicode are not allowed

handle.description:
Unique user handle.

- Length: 4–15 characters
- Allowed characters: `a-z`, `A-Z`, `0-9`, `_`
- Cannot start or end with `_`
- No consecutive underscores (`__`)
- Reserved words are not allowed (e.g. `admin`, `support`, `help`, `system`)

CreateUserResponse.description: Response body returned after a signup request is accepted.

CursorDirection.description: Common cursor direction for bidirectional pagination

DeleteAclGroupRequest.description: Request payload for deleting an ACL group.

group_id.description: ID of the group to delete

DeleteMyAccountRequest.description: Request body for deleting the current account (re-authentication).

password.description: Current password. Required for accounts that have a password set.

totp_code.description:
TOTP code (6 digits) or backup code (8 chars). Required for OAuth-only accounts
that have TOTP enabled.

DeleteNotificationRequest.description: Request payload for delete notification request.

DeviceVerificationRequiredResponse.description: Response body when a new-device email verification is required to finish login.

ForgotPasswordRequest.description: Request body for starting a password reset.

email.description: Email address requesting the password reset

oneOf.description: Cursor direction (default: Older when cursor_id is provided)

cursor_id.description: Cursor ID for pagination. None means get latest.

oneOf.description:
Pagination direction relative to the cursor. This list reads **oldest-first**,
and every page is returned in that order regardless of direction —
`Older`/`Newer` only choose which side of the cursor to read. Defaults to
`Newer` (advance toward newer comments) when a cursor is set; ignored otherwise.

cursor_id.description:
Cursor keyed by a comment id (UUIDv7, time-ordered). Omit to fetch the
first (oldest) page from the start of the thread.

focus_comment_id.description:
Deep-link anchor: return the page window *containing* this comment
instead of paging from a cursor. The comment must belong to the listing
scope (`post_id` + `parent_comment_id`). Mutually exclusive with
`cursor_id`.

parent_comment_id.description: `None` lists top-level comments; `Some(id)` lists the replies under that comment.

GetNotificationsRequest.description: Request payload for get notifications request.

actions.description: Filter by actions (multiple allowed, OR logic)

oneOf.description:
Pagination direction relative to the cursor. This list reads **newest-first**,
and every page is returned in that order regardless of direction —
`Older`/`Newer` only choose which side of the cursor to read. Defaults to
`Older` (advance toward older items) when a cursor is set; ignored otherwise.

cursor_notification_id.description: Cursor notification ID for pagination. None means get latest notifications.

is_read.description: Filter by read status (true = read only, false = unread only)

oneOf.description: Filter by notification type (board, post, user, system)

GetUserProfileByIdRequest.description: Request payload for get user profile by id request.

GetUserProfileRequest.description: Request payload for get user profile request.

GithubLinkRequest.description: Request body for linking a GitHub account to the current user.

code.description: Authorization code from GitHub OAuth callback

GithubLoginRequest.description: Request body for signing in with GitHub OAuth.

code.description: Authorization code from GitHub OAuth callback

GithubTokenRequest.description: Request body for native-app GitHub sign-in with a provider access token.

access_token.description: GitHub OAuth access token obtained by the app via its own in-app authorization.

GoogleLinkRequest.description: Request body for linking a Google account to the current user.

code.description: Authorization code from Google OAuth callback

GoogleLoginRequest.description: Request body for signing in with Google OAuth.

code.description: Authorization code from Google OAuth callback

GoogleOneTapLoginRequest.description: Request body for signing in with Google One Tap.

GoogleOneTapNonceResponse.description: Single-use nonce to pass to Google One Tap initialization.

nonce.description: Single-use nonce bound to the caller's anonymous id (TTL-limited).

GoogleTokenRequest.description: Request body for native-app Google sign-in with a provider ID token.

id_token.description: Google ID token obtained by the app via the native Google SDK.

GrantRoleRequest.description: Request payload for grant role request.

GrantRoleResponse.description: Response payload for grant role response.

ListAclGroupMembersRequest.description: Request payload for listing an ACL group's active members.

cursor_id.description: Cursor: return members older than this member id (newest-first list).

limit.description: Page size — required, matching every other list endpoint's contract.

ListSessionsResponse.description: List of active sessions for the authenticated user.

LoginRequest.description: Request body for email and password login.

email.description: Email address used to sign in.

password.description:
Password for the account. Not length-validated here: login only verifies the
candidate against the stored hash. Length policy is enforced at
signup/change/reset, and a byte cap in the hashing util bounds DoS.

remember_me.description: Whether to stay signed in (30 days if checked; expires when the browser closes if not)

MarkNotificationAsReadRequest.description: Request payload for mark notification as read request.

NotificationAction.description:
Notification action: the concrete reason a notification was produced.

Stored as TEXT in `notification_events.action` (not a Postgres enum) so
the action set can grow without an enum migration.

NotificationActionPreferenceListResponse.description: Response payload for notification action preference list response.

NotificationActionPreferenceResponse.description: Response payload for notification action preference response.

NotificationListResponse.description: Response payload for notification list response.

has_newer.description: Whether there are newer (more recent) notifications

NotificationPreferenceResponse.description: Response payload for notification preference response.

NotificationType.description: Notification type: the notification's top-level category

OAuthAuthorizeFlow.description: Controls whether the generated OAuth authorization URL is used for sign-in or for linking an already authenticated account.

OAuthAuthorizeQuery.description: Query parameters for creating an OAuth authorization URL.

OAuthConnectionListResponse.description: List of OAuth providers linked to the user.

OAuthConnectionResponse.description: One OAuth provider currently linked to the user.

OAuthPendingSignupResponse.description: Response body returned when OAuth sign-in requires profile completion.

email.description: Email received from the OAuth provider

pending_token.description: One-time token for completing the pending signup

OAuthUrlResponse.description: Response body containing a provider authorization URL.

auth_url.description: Google/GitHub OAuth authorization URL (includes state parameter)

PublicUserProfile.description: Response payload for public user profile.

ban_reason.description: Reason recorded for the active ban, if any.

banned_until.description: Ban expiry time. `None` means either not banned or a permanent ban.

deactivated.description: True if the user is deactivated (soft-deleted); handle/display_name stay exposed while the profile is masked.

is_banned.description: True if the user currently has an active (non-expired) ban.

Request payload for replacing a group's permission grants (whole-list

ResendVerificationEmailRequest.description: Request body for resending a pending signup verification email.

token.description: Password reset token (the ?token= value from the email link)

RevokeRoleRequest.description: Request payload for revoke role request.

RevokeRoleResponse.description: Response payload for revoke role response.

query.description: Search query for handle, display_name, or bio. Empty or omitted returns all users.

SessionInfo.description: Active session entry for the authenticated user.

is_current.description: True if this is the session used by the current request

SessionTokenResponse.description: Session token for native-app clients, returned in the response body.

token.description: Opaque session token. Send it back verbatim in the `Authorization: Bearer` header.

SetInitialPasswordRequest.description: Request body for setting the first password on an OAuth-only account.

TotpBackupCodesResponse.description: Response body returned after TOTP backup codes are regenerated.

backup_codes.description: Newly generated backup codes (10 codes, 8-character alphanumeric)

TotpEnableRequest.description: Request body for enabling TOTP after the setup step.

TotpRegenerateBackupCodesRequest.description: Request body for regenerating TOTP backup codes.

TotpRequiredResponse.description: Response body returned when TOTP is required to finish login.

TotpSetupResponse.description: Response body for starting TOTP enrollment.

TotpStatusResponse.description: Response body describing the current TOTP status.

backup_codes_remaining.description: Number of remaining backup codes (only if enabled)

enabled_at.description: When TOTP was enabled (only if enabled)

TotpVerifyRequest.description: Request body for the second step of TOTP login.

code.description: TOTP code (6 digits) or backup code (8 characters)

UnbanUserRequest.description: Request payload for unban user request.

UnbanUserResponse.description: Response payload for unban user response.

UnlinkOAuthRequest.description: Request body for unlinking one OAuth provider from the current user.

provider.description: OAuth provider to unlink (Google or Github)

UnreadCountResponse.description: Response payload for unread count response.

UpdateActionPreferenceRequest.description: Request payload for update action preference request.

UpdateActionPreferencesBulkRequest.description: Request payload for update action preferences bulk request.

UpdateMyProfileRequest.description: Request payload for update my profile request.

display_name.description:
Display name shown in the UI.

- Length: 1–50 characters
- Unicode letters, spaces, and punctuation are permitted
- Emoji, control characters, and invisible Unicode are not allowed

handle.description:
Unique handle used in URLs and mentions.

- Length: 4–15 characters
- ASCII alphanumeric and underscores only
- Cannot start or end with an underscore, no consecutive underscores
- Cannot be a reserved word

UpdateNotificationPreferenceRequest.description: Request payload for update notification preference request.

UploadUserImageRequest.description: Request payload for upload user image request.

file.description: Image file binary data. Supported input formats: JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP. Stored output is sanitized WebP.

has_password.description: Whether the account has a password set (false for OAuth-only accounts)

token.description: The single-use token delivered to the account's email address.

VerifyEmailRequest.description: Request body for completing an email signup.
